title: The Mask Behind the Mask

activity.name: Musée des Explorations du Monde — Exterior Investigation & Cipher Decode
activity.address: Place de la Castre, Le Suquet, 06400 Cannes, France

narrative_intro: The climb up to the Castre plateau has left your calves burning, but it's your chest that's tight. You're standing where history locked a man away and erased his name so thoroughly that three centuries of scholars couldn't recover it. The Musée des Explorations du Monde — housed inside the medieval castle of the Cannes monks — sits before you, its 11th-century tower rising like a finger pressed to stone lips. Somewhere inside these walls, according to the cipher map you've been chasing, a set of documents was sealed behind masonry during renovations in 1698, the same month the Iron Mask prisoner was transferred to the Bastille. But Vera's map is a forgery. You know that now. Which means either the documents don't exist at all... or Vera reverse-engineered her fake from something real. The museum's exterior holds the answer. The original castle walls bear mason's marks — stonemason signatures carved during medieval construction and again during 17th-century restorations. If the cipher map's coordinate system matches the actual pattern of mason's marks on the tower, Vera didn't invent the map. She copied it from a source she hasn't told you about. Your expertise as an authenticator comes down to this: read the stones.
instruction: Make your way to the base of the Tour du Suquet (the square medieval tower beside the Musée des Explorations du Monde). Photograph the tower's stone facade, focusing on the lower sections where original mason's marks and restoration seams are visible. Count the distinct mason's marks you can identify on the visible face of the tower and report your findings to Noor. Then photograph the panoramic view of the Vieux Port from the Castre esplanade — you need to confirm whether the seized yacht Vera described is actually visible at the quay.
player_action: Photograph the stone facade of the Tour du Suquet tower, identify and count visible mason's marks or carved symbols on the lower stones, then photograph the panoramic view of the Vieux Port below to verify the presence of a large yacht at the quay. Report both findings to Noor via the app.

gps_trigger.content_brief: A high-resolution scan of a 1698 inventory ledger from the Lérins monastery archives, listing 'one reliquary, brass and bone, contents sealed by order of M. de Saint-Mars' — the real jailer of the Iron Mask. A margin note in different ink reads: 'The marks on the Castre tower correspond. Third face, seventh course.' Le Pharmacien has annotated the scan: 'Vera found this ledger in 2019. She never told anyone. Now you know where her map came from. The question is what she ADDED to the original. —P'

camera_prompt: The player photographs the medieval Tour du Suquet tower facade. AI should identify the old stone construction, any visible carved marks or symbols in the stonework, restoration seams between different eras of masonry, and the general condition of the medieval walls. The AI character (Noor) will interpret these marks as potential cipher reference points, comparing them to the map's coordinate system. Second photo: panoramic view of the Vieux Port from above — AI identifies boats, yachts, the quay layout, and any notably large vessel that could be the seized oligarch yacht.

tension.description: Three truths collide simultaneously: Le Pharmacien's leaked document proves Vera based her forgery on a real 1698 source, Noor realizes the player now has enough information to authenticate OR discredit the entire cipher map, and Yael Morin demands the player share everything before the buyer's yacht departs. The player must decide whose version of events to validate — and every choice closes a door permanently.
tension.resolution: The player's analysis of the mason's marks and their report to Noor determines whether the historical documents are treated as real (triggering a race to the final location) or fabricated (triggering a confrontation with Vera). Either path leads to Step 6, but the alliances are radically different.

[pause] I need you to understand something. Whatever you find in Step 6 — the historical documents, the evidence — I don't care about the Carlton network or Vera's justice crusade. I care about my father's name. Send me the port photo next. And then you need to decide whose team you're playing for, because I just ran Vera Solari through Europol. She has THREE prior investigations for evidence fabrication. Three. So either your friends are manufacturing urgency to keep you running, or that crew is about to commit a maritime violation I can finally arrest someone for. I need you to look down at the port from where you are. Tell me if you see deck activity — tenders, crew movement, anything. And while you're deciding who to trust: I ran Vera Solari through Europol. verification.target: Mason's marks on Tour du Suquet facade + panoramic port view with yacht identification
verification.success_condition: Player photographs the tower showing visible carved marks or stone patterns AND provides a count of marks (any reasonable number between 5-20) AND photographs the port panorama showing vessels at quay
verification.success_reaction: Noor confirms the player's count aligns closely with her father's 2003 survey, granting +30 trust with Noor and unlocking her father's complete field notes in the vault — a 47-page document that contains the REAL cipher key, independent of Vera's reconstruction. This gives the player an authentication advantage in Step 6. Noor whispers: 'You just did what an entire review board refused to do. You confirmed his work. I won't forget this.' Yael Morin also gains +15 trust if the player shared the port photo with her, as she can now dispatch harbor police.
verification.failure_fallback: If the player cannot identify mason's marks or provides no count, Noor is disappointed but sends her father's field notes anyway with a colder tone: 'Fine. The marks are subtle — my father needed specialized lighting to document most of them. I'll send you his survey. Use it in Step 6. But I needed YOU to see them. Not just read about them.' The story continues to Step 6 with reduced trust from Noor (-15) but the same destination.
verification.timeout_reaction: After 6 minutes, Dimitri sends an uncharacteristic message: '⏱️ The aft tender just hit the water. Move or don't. Your choice has a shelf life.' Vera follows 30 seconds later: 'Third buttress. Western wall. NOW.' The player is directed to Step 6 regardless.

unlock_message: Le Pharmacien has dropped a document into your vault. The monastery ledger changes everything — Vera's map isn't invention, it's reconstruction. But reconstruction from what? The tower holds the answer in its stones.
